chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Let us know the difference between greedy method and dynamic programming which is very

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: very important.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Greedy method and dynamic programming both are used for solving optimization problems

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: both are different strategies but the purpose is same.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Optimization problems are those which requires either minimum result or maximum result.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The methods are different in greedy method we try to follow predefined procedure to get

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the optimal result.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: The procedure is known to be optimal we follow the procedure to get best result like Kruskal's

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: method for finding minimum cost spanning tree always select a minimum cost edge and that

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: gives us best result or Dijkstra shortest path algorithm always select the shortest

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: path vertex and continue relaxing the vertices so you get the shortest path.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So there is a predefined procedure but in dynamic programming we will try to find out

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: all possible solutions and then pick up the best solution the optimal solution.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This approach is different and it is little time consuming compared to greedy method.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: For any problem there may be many solutions which are feasible so we will try out all

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: those solutions and then pick up the best one and mostly dynamic programming problems

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: are solved by using recursive formulas though we will not use recursion of programming but

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: the formulas are recursive if we want we can use recursion also but mostly they are solved

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: using iteration and dynamic programming follows principle of optimality.

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Principle of optimality says that a problem can be solved by taking sequence of decisions

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari

chunks.title: Principle  of Optimality  - Dynamic Programming introduction - Abdul Bari

text: Hi the topic is dynamic programming in this video I'll give you introduction to dynamic programming give you differences between dynamic programming and greedy method and also I will show the difference between memoization and tabular method by taking an example in detail. Let us know the difference between greedy method and dynamic programming which is very very important. Greedy method and dynamic programming both are used for solving optimization problems both are different strategies but the purpose is same. Optimization problems are those which requires either minimum result or maximum result. The methods are different in greedy method we try to follow predefined procedure to get the optimal result. The procedure is known to be optimal we follow the procedure to get best result like Kruskal's method for finding minimum cost spanning tree always select a minimum cost edge and that gives us best result or Dijkstra shortest path algorithm always select the shortest path vertex and continue relaxing the vertices so you get the shortest path. So there is a predefined procedure but in dynamic programming we will try to find out all possible solutions and then pick up the best solution the optimal solution. This approach is different and it is little time consuming compared to greedy method. For any problem there may be many solutions which are feasible so we will try out all those solutions and then pick up the best one and mostly dynamic programming problems are solved by using recursive formulas though we will not use recursion of programming but the formulas are recursive if we want we can use recursion also but mostly they are solved using iteration and dynamic programming follows principle of optimality. Principle of optimality says that a problem can be solved by taking sequence of decisions to get the optimal solution. So now I can highlight one difference here in greedy method decision is taken one time that we will do it like this and we follow that procedure. In dynamic programming every stage we take a decision so this point you can understand only while solving the problem so while solving the problem I will show you perfectly how dynamic programming is working there to solve a problem. Let us see how dynamic programming adopts tabulation method or memoization difference between tabulation and memoization. I have an example function here this is the recursive definition of a Fibonacci term and this is a function or an algorithm for finding Fibonacci term and it is a recursive function the definition itself is recursive so the function is recursive. So what is Fibonacci series it starts from 0 and 1 1 every term is obtained by adding previous two terms so 2 plus 1 that is 3 and 2 plus 3 that is 5 and 5 plus 3 that is 8 and this is 13 and so on so this is 0th term first term second third fourth fifth sixth seventh and goes on. Suppose I want to know 10th term of Fibonacci series then call this function this function will give us 10th term. Now I will show you how this function works if I want to find out any term because the function is recursive so let us trace this function when I call this function for Fibonacci of 5th term I want to find out if I trace this n is less than equal to 1 no it is not less than equal to 1 if so then it will return n only otherwise it will call two functions add their results and return the result so what are those two functions it is calling it is calling itself for Fibonacci of 3 and Fibonacci of 4. So first of all it will call this one right then that one so if we see this again it will enter inside and then it's not less than equal to 1 so it will call itself for 2 times for 3 minus 2 that is Fib of 1 and then Fib of 2 then out of this it will pick up this call first so Fib of 1 directly we get the answer 1 so this is 1 then it will come this side so there are two calls here this is Fib of 0 and Fib of 1 for Fib of 0 and Fib of 1 there is no recursive call direct result is return so here the functions terminates then it will add these two terms and give the result it will add these two terms and give the result then this side it has to continue so on this side again for Fibonacci of 4 it will call itself for Fibonacci of 2 and this side for Fibonacci of 3 but first of all this is done so this is Fib of 0 and Fib of 1 this side Fib of 1 and Fib of 2 again this is Fib of 0 and Fib of 1 this will be the tracing tree of that particular function so for finding Fib of 5 it is making total 15 calls now if I consider all the calls and find out the time complexity of this one so from the function if I see this is n minus 2 and that is n minus 1 if I assume roughly it is n minus 1 only then I can prepare a recurrence relation for this one what it will be tn is equals to it is calling itself for two time let us assume n minus 1 only so 2 tn minus 1 though this is n minus 2 but if we take n minus 2 we cannot come to the conclusion so I am taking it as 1 right approximately so this is 2 tn minus 1 and what it is doing every time just adding so this is the recurrence relation and by master's theorem for decreasing function if you take this is big O of 2 power n like this is not exact it is n minus 2 it is less I have taken n minus 1 only so this upper bound for that function so the time taken by this function is big O of 2 power n right recurrence relation how to prepare there is a video available you can watch that one so how to prepare a recurrence relation so this recurrence relation is big O of 2 power n means if I call this Fibonacci function it will take so much time 2 power n exponential time is there any way to reduce the time let us observe see in this function F of 1 fib of 1 1 1 so many times it is being called so many times so if I take fib of 2 it is called here and here and here why to call the same function again and again for the same parameter why can't we reduce this one so yes with this idea we can reduce the function calls and reduce the time taken by this Fibonacci function so what I'll do is I'll try to store the result of Fibonacci function so that once it is called for fib of 2 it should not be called again so let me show you how it can be done we will take one global array let us say I have an array 0 1 2 3 4 5 I have an array initially I will fill this array with minus 1 because nothing is known now let me trace this from tree once again not function I have to modify the function but I will not modify it just
